Description: Users need immediate feedback when actions are processing to know the system is responsive
Do: Show loading indicators within 100ms of user action
Don't: Leave users wondering if their click registered

Description: Users need confirmation that their action completed successfully to feel confident
Do: Show clear success message or visual feedback after completing actions
Don't: Silently complete action without any acknowledgment

Description: Generic error messages frustrate users and prevent them from resolving issues
Do: Provide specific actionable error messages that explain what went wrong and how to fix it
Don't: Show technical jargon or generic error occurred messages
Code Example Good: <Error>Email is already registered. Try signing in instead.</Error>

Description: Long operations without progress feedback cause users to abandon or retry
Do: Show progress bars with percentage or step indicators for operations over 3 seconds
Don't: Show only a spinner for lengthy operations

Description: Users perceive delay over 100ms and become uncertain if under 1 second with no feedback
Do: Provide immediate visual feedback even if data takes time to load
Don't: Wait for data before showing any response

Description: Users need to know where they are in the app hierarchy to navigate confidently
Do: Highlight current page in navigation show breadcrumbs for deep pages
Don't: Leave users guessing their current location

Description: Breaking browser back button behavior frustrates users who rely on it
Do: Ensure back button works correctly especially for modals and multi-step flows
Don't: Trap users in modal states or break history

Description: Important navigation hidden behind hamburger reduces discoverability and engagement
Do: Use bottom navigation for primary actions on mobile keep top 4-5 items visible
Don't: Hide all navigation behind hamburger menu

Description: Users get lost in deep hierarchies without breadcrumbs or quick navigation options
Do: Provide breadcrumbs quick links or recent items for deeply nested content
Don't: Force users through every level to reach content

Description: Users learn navigation patterns and inconsistency forces relearning
Do: Keep navigation position and behavior consistent across the entire app
Don't: Move navigation or change patterns between sections

Description: Users need immediate feedback about input validity to correct errors efficiently
Do: Validate on blur and show inline feedback immediately
Don't: Only validate on submit leaving users to guess issues

Description: Users waste time submitting forms only to learn about required fields
Do: Mark required fields clearly before user attempts submission
Don't: Show required errors only after failed submission

Description: Complex fields without guidance cause user confusion and errors
Do: Provide helper text tooltips or examples for non-obvious fields
Don't: Assume users understand all field requirements
Code Example Good: <Input helperText="Password must be 8+ characters with a number" />

Description: Users lose trust and patience when their entered data disappears unexpectedly
Do: Auto-save drafts warn before navigation confirm before clear
Don't: Clear form data on navigation without warning
Code Example Bad: // no save user refreshes loses all

Description: Long forms overwhelm users and increase abandonment
Do: Break long forms into logical steps with progress indication
Don't: Show 20 fields on single scrolling page

Description: Power users expect tab navigation and keyboard shortcuts for efficiency
Do: Ensure proper tab order and allow form submission with Enter key
Don't: Force mouse use for every interaction

Description: Screen reader users cannot understand images without alternative text descriptions
Do: Provide descriptive alt text for all meaningful images
Don't: Leave alt empty or use generic image

Description: Users with visual impairments cannot read text with insufficient contrast
Do: Maintain minimum 4.5:1 contrast ratio for normal text 3:1 for large text
Don't: Use light gray text on white backgrounds

Description: Keyboard users cannot navigate when focus indicators are removed
Do: Provide clear visible focus states for all interactive elements
Don't: Remove focus outlines for aesthetics

Description: Screen reader users must listen to entire navigation on every page
Do: Provide skip to main content link at top of page
Don't: Force users through all navigation content

Description: Users who cannot use mouse are excluded from hover-only features
Do: Ensure all hover features have keyboard and touch alternatives
Don't: Rely on hover for essential functionality
Code Example Good: Show tooltip on focus and hover

Description: Screen readers cannot identify unlabeled form fields
Do: Associate every input with a label using for attribute or wrapping
Don't: Use placeholder as only field identification

Description: Users abandon pages that take over 3 seconds to become interactive
Do: Optimize for under 1.5s LCP prioritize above-fold content
Don't: Load everything before showing anything useful

Description: Content jumping around frustrates users and causes mis-clicks
Do: Reserve space for dynamic content specify image dimensions
Don't: Allow content to shift as images and ads load

Description: Users cannot interact while heavy operations block the main thread
Do: Use web workers for heavy computation keep main thread free
Don't: Run expensive calculations on main thread

Description: Users lose access to content and work when connectivity drops
Do: Cache critical content and enable offline functionality
Don't: Fail completely without network

Description: Fetching data users do not need wastes bandwidth and time
Do: Fetch only required data implement pagination
Don't: Fetch entire dataset when showing subset

Description: New users feel lost without introduction to key features
Do: Provide contextual onboarding tooltips or guided tour for new users
Don't: Drop users into complex interface without help

Description: Too much information at once causes cognitive overload and abandonment
Do: Introduce features progressively as users need them
Don't: Show all features and options immediately

Description: Blank screens without guidance leave users confused about next steps
Do: Show helpful empty states with clear calls to action
Don't: Display blank areas where content should be
Code Example Good: <EmptyState icon={<Plus />} title="No projects yet" action="Create project" />

Description: Forcing signup before value demonstration causes immediate abandonment
Do: Allow users to experience core value before requiring signup
Don't: Require account creation to see any content
Code Example Good: Allow browsing with signup prompt for saving
Code Example Bad: Login required to view any page

Description: Users lose progress on long flows when returning later
Do: Save progress and allow users to resume where they left off
Don't: Require completion in single session
Code Example Bad: // must complete all steps at once

Description: Users spend more time typing and make more errors without suggestions
Do: Show real-time suggestions as user types
Don't: Require full query before showing results

Description: Dead-end no results pages cause users to give up
Do: Suggest alternatives check spelling or show related content
Don't: Show empty results with no guidance

Description: Users repeat common searches unnecessarily without history
Do: Show recent searches and allow quick re-search
Don't: Clear history on every search

Description: Users do not know what content their search will cover
Do: Indicate search scope and allow filtering
Don't: Search unclear areas without indication

Description: Users expect near-instant search results under 200ms
Do: Implement search indexing and optimize queries
Don't: Search unindexed database on every keystroke
Code Example Good: Use search service like Algolia Elasticsearch
Code Example Bad: Raw SQL LIKE query on full table

Description: Small targets cause frustration and mis-taps especially on the go
Do: Make touch targets at least 44x44px with adequate spacing
Don't: Use desktop-sized 20px buttons on mobile

Description: Users expect pull to refresh for content feeds on mobile
Do: Implement pull to refresh for refreshable content lists
Don't: Force users to find refresh button

Description: Desktop hover states and layouts do not translate to touch
Do: Design for touch first with appropriate mobile patterns
Don't: Shrink desktop layout for mobile

Description: Inputs hidden behind keyboard prevent users from seeing what they type
Do: Scroll inputs into view when keyboard appears
Don't: Allow keyboard to cover active input

Description: Touch interfaces without haptic feedback feel unresponsive
Do: Add subtle haptic feedback for important interactions
Don't: No physical feedback on touch

Description: Large blocks of text overwhelm users and reduce comprehension
Do: Break content into scannable chunks with headings and bullets
Don't: Present long paragraphs without formatting

Description: Users do not know what to do when CTAs are vague or hidden
Do: Use clear action-oriented button labels make CTAs prominent
Don't: Use generic Submit or Click here labels

Description: Users cannot quickly scan and find relevant information
Do: Use clear visual hierarchy with headings subheadings body
Don't: All text same size and weight

Description: Stale content erodes trust and causes user confusion
Do: Show last updated dates keep content current
Don't: Display outdated information without indication
Code Example Bad: // no date could be years old

Description: Technical terms alienate users unfamiliar with domain
Do: Explain or link technical terms provide glossary
Don't: Use unexplained acronyms and jargon

Description: Users hesitate to commit without evidence others have succeeded
Do: Show reviews testimonials or user counts
Don't: Expect users to trust without evidence

Description: Users distrust sites that hide pricing until late in funnel
Do: Show pricing clearly and early in user journey
Don't: Require signup or sales call to see pricing

Description: Users lose trust when they cannot reach support if needed
Do: Provide clear contact options and response time expectations
Don't: Hide support or make it difficult to find

Description: Users hesitate to enter sensitive data without security indicators
Do: Show security badges SSL indicators privacy assurances
Don't: No security indication on sensitive forms

Description: Users worry about data use without clear privacy information
Do: Explain data use clearly link to privacy policy
Don't: Collect data without explaining use

Description: Preventable errors frustrate users and waste time
Do: Validate inputs disable invalid actions confirm destructive operations
Don't: Allow users to make easily preventable mistakes

Description: Users stuck on errors without recovery options abandon tasks
Do: Provide clear recovery paths retry options or alternatives
Don't: Show error without way to recover

Description: Lost work due to errors destroys user trust and causes abandonment
Do: Auto-save drafts preserve state on errors allow recovery
Don't: Clear form or lose data on error

Description: Technical errors confuse non-technical users and prevent resolution
Do: Translate technical errors to user-friendly explanations
Don't: Show stack traces or error codes to users
Code Example Good: <Error>Unable to save. Please try again or contact support.</Error>

Description: Network errors without explanation cause user confusion
Do: Explain connection issues and provide offline options
Don't: Fail silently or show generic error
Code Example Good: <OfflineNotice>You're offline. Changes will sync when connected.</OfflineNotice>

Description: Different terms for same concept confuse users
Do: Use consistent terminology throughout the application
Don't: Use delete remove trash interchangeably
Code Example Good: Always use Delete for removal actions

Description: Visual inconsistency makes hierarchy and patterns unclear
Do: Use consistent button hierarchy primary secondary tertiary
Don't: Mix styles randomly throughout app
Code Example Good: Define and follow button style system

Description: Users must relearn interactions when patterns change
Do: Keep interaction patterns consistent across features
Don't: Different patterns for similar features
Code Example Good: Same edit flow in all sections

Description: Users expect platform conventions and violations cause friction
Do: Follow platform conventions for navigation gestures patterns
Don't: Ignore platform standards
Code Example Good: iOS back gesture Android back button work correctly
Code Example Bad: Custom gestures that conflict with platform

Description: Inconsistent feedback patterns create uncertainty
Do: Use same feedback patterns throughout app
Don't: Different loading error success patterns
Code Example Good: Same toast style and position everywhere

Description: One-size-fits-all interfaces frustrate users with different needs
Do: Allow customization of key preferences
Don't: Force same experience on all users

Description: Requiring repeated choices wastes user time
Do: Remember user choices and preferences across sessions
Don't: Ask same questions every session

Description: Poor defaults force users to configure on every use
Do: Set smart defaults based on user context
Don't: Start with empty or irrelevant defaults

Description: Different user segments have different needs
Do: Adapt experience based on user type or behavior
Don't: Show same experience to beginner and expert

Description: Users feel trapped without ability to export their data
Do: Allow users to export their data in standard formats
Don't: Lock users into platform with no export

Description: Manual repetition of common tasks wastes user time
Do: Automate common patterns provide bulk actions
Don't: Require repetitive manual work
Code Example Bad: // must do one at a time

Description: Power users cannot work efficiently without shortcuts
Do: Provide keyboard shortcuts for common actions
Don't: Mouse required for all actions
Code Example Good: Cmd+K for search Cmd+S for save

Description: Excessive steps for simple tasks frustrate users
Do: Minimize steps required for common actions
Don't: Require unnecessary confirmations and clicks

Description: Starting from scratch every time wastes user time
Do: Provide useful defaults and templates
Don't: Always start with blank state

Description: Inability to undo causes fear of making mistakes
Do: Provide undo for destructive or significant actions
Don't: Immediate permanent destructive actions

Description: Excessive options cause decision paralysis
Do: Limit choices provide defaults hide advanced options
Don't: Show all possible options at once

Description: Unnecessary complexity increases cognitive load
Do: Use simple clear language explain necessary complexity
Don't: Use jargon when plain language would work

Description: Too much information prevents comprehension
Do: Show essential information progressive disclosure for details
Don't: Show all data fields at once

Description: Users cannot understand how elements relate without visual cues
Do: Use visual hierarchy grouping and spacing to show relationships
Don't: Flat design with no visual hierarchy
Code Example Good: Group related items with spacing borders

Description: Users cannot quickly find information without visual aids
Do: Use headings icons colors to support scanning
Don't: Dense text without visual breaks
Code Example Good: Clear sections with icons and headers

Description: Manual typing of known values wastes time and causes errors
Do: Auto-complete for known values like addresses
Don't: Require full manual entry

Description: Users guess at required formats causing validation errors
Do: Show expected format with examples
Don't: Reject input without format explanation

Description: Forcing unnecessary data collection creates friction
Do: Only require fields truly necessary
Don't: Require optional data as mandatory

Description: Empty fields require more user effort to complete
Do: Pre-fill with intelligent defaults
Don't: Start with empty fields
Code Example Good: Pre-fill country from IP timezone from browser

Description: Disabling paste forces manual entry and frustrates users
Do: Allow paste for all text fields
Don't: Disable paste on password or verification fields

Description: Notification overload causes users to ignore all notifications
Do: Limit notifications to important actionable items
Don't: Notify for every minor event

Description: Confusing notifications waste user attention
Do: Clear notification title and actionable content
Don't: Vague notification requiring investigation

Description: Inability to control notifications frustrates users
Do: Let users control notification preferences
Don't: All or nothing notification settings

Description: Interrupting users at wrong times creates negative experience
Do: Send notifications at appropriate times respect quiet hours
Don't: Notify at 3am about non-urgent matters

Description: Persistent notifications without clear dismissal annoy users
Do: Provide clear way to dismiss or act on notifications
Don't: Sticky notifications without close

Description: Users cannot find help relevant to their current context
Do: Provide help relevant to current screen or feature
Don't: Generic FAQ for all questions

Description: Users cannot find answers when help is not searchable
Do: Make help content searchable
Don't: Browsing only help documentation

Description: Outdated help causes more confusion than no help
Do: Keep documentation current with product
Don't: Help describes old features

Description: Forcing support contact for simple issues wastes everyone time
Do: Enable self-service for common issues
Don't: Support ticket for password reset

Description: Users cannot get help when they cannot find it
Do: Make help easily accessible from any screen
Don't: Help buried in settings

Description: Missing expected social features reduce engagement
Do: Add social features appropriate to context
Don't: No community features in social product
Code Example Bad: // social app with no social features

Description: Requiring social actions for basic functionality frustrates users
Do: Make social features optional
Don't: Force share to unlock features

Description: Lack of privacy controls prevents users from sharing
Do: Let users control visibility and sharing
Don't: Public by default no privacy options

Description: Users need protection from harassment and abuse
Do: Enable blocking and reporting bad actors
Don't: No way to stop harassment

Description: Unmoderated content can harm users and brand
Do: Implement content moderation and community guidelines
Don't: Allow any content without review

Description: Single locale excludes international users
Do: Support multiple locales currencies timezones
Don't: English USD only

Description: Bad translations confuse users and appear unprofessional
Do: Use professional translations test with native speakers
Don't: Machine translate without review
